summary: The default branch retains the existing independent push-triggered Docker publisher. It has no exact-SHA required-results gate, still reruns frontend/generated-contract validation with Python and Node setup, performs no image-level smoke verification, and its contract tests assert the duplicate validation remains. Existing production frontend construction inside the Dockerfile and digest-based multiarchitecture publication satisfy only portions of the issue. No recent merge referencing #3328 or its distinctive title was found. No long test suite was run because the decisive workflow and contract-test evidence is static and directly inspectable.

requirements.description: No app-image build or publication job starts until the same commit SHA has successful ci-required, test-frontend, check-generated-contracts, and migration-gate results; missing, cancelled, stale, or mismatched-SHA evidence fails closed.
The approved immutable SHA is passed explicitly into checkout and image metadata.
Docker publishing no longer reruns frontend typecheck, lint, frontend unit tests, or generated-contract validation, and removes Python/Node setup used only by those checks.
The production frontend assets remain built inside the Docker image.

The exact image to be published receives a bounded image-level smoke verification covering startup/entrypoint, Vite assets, build ID and OCI revision, runtime binaries, and non-empty per-platform digest.
Multiarchitecture images continue to be assembled and published from per-platform digests.
Package-write permissions are confined to trusted exact-commit post-CI jobs.
Workflow summaries record the approved SHA and verified required-result check/run evidence.
The Docker build remains separate from cross-workflow dashboard-dist consumption.

Workflow contract tests enforce the exact-SHA gate, approved SHA propagation, image smoke verification, trusted package permissions, and absence of duplicate validation/setup while preserving digest publication.
The selected exact-SHA publication orchestration model is documented.
